summary: Installed alpha-skill into QA Claude project by symlink; UI, DB, and readlink agree; host skill remained intact.
Removed installed symlink from QA project; project target disappeared and host alpha-skill remained.
No switch-mode UI/action or install mode parameter exists in the current dev surface, so the case cannot be executed as written.
Install conflict failed with conflict_error; unmanaged target remained unchanged and no current managed install was created.
Reset cleared metadata and preserved fixture folders, but Electron relaunched as a detached blank window instead of returning to first-run/setup state.
Configured QA host via renderer RPC bridge, scanned host, and verified Settings/DB/filesystem point to the QA fixture host.
After moving the configured host folder out of the way, Dashboard still showed status active, stale skill/project counts, and no visible recovery warning.
Changed active host to QA host B; Settings/Skills Library/DB showed gamma-skill from host B while host A and host B remained intact.
QA project appears in Projects and Project Detail; DB provider facts show Claude detected under the QA project path.
Manual project scan completed and updated last_scanned_at; recent operation records show one scan operation for the manual action.
Skills Library and DB list alpha-skill and beta-skill from the active QA host; filesystem contains both fixture skills.
Broken project symlink scanned as broken_symlink with warning evidence; UI remained stable and showed the broken entry.
Global Plugins scan shows Claude QA fixture plugins, statuses, versions, marketplace metadata, and safe fixture paths.
Toggled qa-plugin off and back on in QA settings; only fixture settings were touched and final status/version returned to expected state.
Project Detail shows user/project/local plugin layers and effective state; Global Plugins remains a user-layer view.
Malformed QA settings file scanned as malformed, app stayed stable, and checksum evidence shows the malformed file was not overwritten.
No plugin update-check operation or update cache rows appeared before explicit manual action; manual network check was not executed because no outbound approval was requested.
Selected because it is T1, but this run targeted dev Electron and no current packaged macOS artifact/test app-data setup was provided.
